chunk_summary: Oral health counselling for complementary feeding (6–24 months) emphasizes reducing sugary exposures, stopping bottle use by 12 months, and promoting fluoride toothpaste and dental visits to prevent early childhood caries.
learning_objectives: Understand the link between feeding practices and oral health in toddlers.
Learn strategies to prevent early childhood caries, including reducing sugary snacks and bottle use.
Implement fluoride toothpaste use and supervised toothbrushing for children under 3 years.
Promote early dental visits and tooth-friendly snack alternatives.
key_facts: Prolonged bottle use and frequent sugary snacks increase risk of early childhood caries.
Stop bottle and teat use by 12 months; shift milk to an open cup.
Use a rice-grain sized smear of fluoride toothpaste twice daily for children under 3 years.
Encourage a first dental check by age 1 year or after the first tooth erupts.
Sugary foods and drinks should be excluded from the daily routine under 2 years.
Repeated small sugary exposures between meals are high risk for caries.
reference_quote: For children under 3 years, use a tiny smear (rice-grain sized) of fluoride toothpaste twice daily with adult supervision to limit swallowing. — American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry
